title: [Bug]: Can not reach exposed port if container is attached to two networks
body:
### Issue Description

Can not connect from the outside to a exported port if the container is attached to two networks

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Crate two networks:
   `podman network  create --driver bridge --gateway "100.65.3.1" --subnet "100.65.3.0/24" --ipv6 --gateway "fdfb:a91c:79de:6501::1" --subnet "fdfb:a91c:79de:6501::/64" example`
    `podman network  create --driver bridge --gateway "100.65.0.1" --subnet "100.65.0.0/24" --ipv6 --gateway "fdfb:991c:79de:6500::1" --subnet "fdfb:991c:79de:6500::/64" example2`
3. Start a container that is attached to both networks and expose a port:
   `podman run --rm -p 7171:80 --network example,example2 --name frontend docker.io/nginx`

5.  Try to curl:
   `curl 192.168.xxx.xxx:7171`

It is very important to note that you must use a ip. `localhost` will work. If you attach the container only to one network, everything works fine


### Describe the results you received

Can not connect to the exposed port

### Describe the results you expected

Should be able to connect to the exposed port

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Can you check what value `sysctl net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter` is set? And set it to 2 if it is one. `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=2`. Then make sure to test again.

I sometimes have this issue - though I can't figure out the exact conditions to reproduce it.
On a "clean" test machine, I haven't been able to reproduce this behavior - however, on my personal machine, I see it occur.

On both machines `net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter` is set to 2 - systemd sets that as a default on my distro.

I'm using rootful containers - and I've noticed that, unlike OP, only IPv6 is inaccessible for me - I haven't had a chance to see if containers/netavark#638 fixes it in my setup, but these containers are using the standard `-p PORT:PORT` syntax, so that shouldn't play a role.

I'll keep fiddling - once I get more details, I'll try to help troubleshoot.
Never heard back so I am going to close this.
